sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The only difference is the white bar at the bottom changed from 7 pixels to 8 pixels (boundary from [63, 57] to [63, 56]). This is likely a HUD/timer/progress bar, not a gameplay change. Clicking on the frame didn't affect the gameplay objects.
So clicking on frames doesn't do anything meaningful. The only meaningful interactions are:
1. LEFT/RIGHT moves the left green square horizontally
2. Clicking a green square swaps the center colors (clicked→W, other→c)
Let me reconsider the goal. Maybe I need to:
1. Move the left green square to the left frame
2. Move the right green square to the right frame
3. Have both centers match (both W or both c)
But the mechanic only allows one center to be W at a time. Unless... maybe the goal is to have the centers match the frame interiors. The frames have white interiors, so maybe I need both centers to be W. But that's not possible with the current mechanic.
Wait, maybe I need to think about this differently. The right green square is already near the right frame. Maybe I need to move it INTO the frame. Let me check if the green squares can move vertically.
Earlier, UP didn't work. But maybe UP only works when the green square is in a certain position. Let me try moving the left green square to the left frame position and then try UP/DOWN.
Actually, let me first check if LEFT moves the left green square back.
